canto_title: The Vision of the Celestial Order and Beatrice's Instruction

scenes.summary: Dante declares his intention to sing of the divine glory that moves the universe.
The poet prays to Apollo for the poetic inspiration and breath necessary to describe the blessed kingdom.
Dante reflects on the difficulty of his task and the hope for a more successful voice in the future.
A description of the celestial light and the perfection of the cosmic structure.
Dante witnesses Beatrice turning to look at the sun, pulling his gaze toward her.
The intense brilliance of the celestial light overwhelms the poet's vision like glowing iron.
Dante and Beatrice gaze at one another within the eternal spheres.
Dante finds himself unable to express the concept of 'transhumanizing' through mere words.
The cosmic music and light ignite a profound and unprecedented desire within the poet.
Beatrice speaks to correct Dante's false perceptions and clarify his true location.
Dante expresses his astonishment at the realization that he has transcended his earthly body.
Beatrice explains how all things in the universe are ordered to reflect the divine.
Beatrice describes how all creatures and elements are moved through the heavens by instinct and intellect.
The poet learns how Divine Providence maintains the stability and purpose of the celestial motions.
Beatrice explains how certain forces can deviate from the divine path due to false pleasure.
Beatrice concludes her lesson by comparing Dante's ascent to a descending stream and turns back to the sky.
